mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, rising industrial production reflects accommodative financing conditions translating into higher factory output, which quickly prices into equity markets as positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02 specifically during documented easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >60% of high-inflation trigger months in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity and corporate profit margins, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters with positive GDP growth over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity, which immediately raises recession fears and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns dominate (>60%) following large industrial production drops in out-of-sample validation.
